column3: The old plane sat inside a dusty **hangar**.
Ground crew worked on the busy **apron** area.
The jet moved slowly along the **taxiway**.
The initial **ascent** was surprisingly smooth and quick.
The **descent** began forty minutes before landing.
Please print your **itinerary** before reaching the airport.
I had to **rebook** my morning to Paris.
She waited on **standby** for an earlier trip.
Several **unclaimed** bags sat near the office.
Our plane was **diverted** due to bad weather.
The crew was **rerouted** through Frankfurt last night.
Please wait to **disembark** until the doors open.
The chair in front began to **recline** suddenly.
Place your small backpack in the **overhead** compartment.
Please **stow** your laptop before we depart.
Passengers must **fasten** their belts during takeoff.

Everyone must pass the **screening** before the gates.

The **rudder** helps control the direction of flying.

Strong **thrust** pushed everyone back into their chairs.
A loud **turbine** hummed outside my tiny window.
This **aircraft** carries three hundred people easily.

The smooth **touchdown** earned applause from the cabin.
The **gatecrew** announced boarding would start soon.
A loud **announcement** told everyone about the delay.
A **paging** message called for a missing traveler.
Most people sit in **coachclass** on long trips.

He loves a **windowside** spot for the views.

The **bulkhead** row offers extra space for families.
A **bassinet** can be requested for babies onboard.

She packed her small **duffel** with flying essentials.
Each person gets one free **carryon** piece today.

The **groundcrew** signaled the plane out of position.
A **marshaller** guided the jet with lighted sticks.
Each **chock** keeps the plane from rolling away.
A small **tugtruck** pushed the plane from the gates.

**Refueling** takes place before every long flight.

We reached **cruise** height above the thick clouds.
The jet flew at **mach** point seven today.

A free **shuttlebus** runs between the main buildings.
